name: National Charter Bus Fort WRTH
owner_or_parent: National Charter Bus (national broker/marketplace, HQ Atlanta)
principals: Not a local Fort Worth operator; Hannah Mack data field is incorrect (she is CEO of Colleyville Chamber of Commerce, not this company)
districts_or_customers: Corporate, wedding and educational group charters across DFW metro; no evidence of school district home-to-school contracts
recent_news: No M&A news. Operates as a virtual/local landing page of National Charter Bus, a nationwide charter broker/marketplace that subcontracts to local motorcoach operators.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate. This is a national charter broker/marketplace landing page, not a school-bus contractor with a physical Fort Worth yard or fleet. Drop from target list.

name: Trans4kids Shuttle Inc
districts_or_customers: Day-care, school and after-school child shuttle in Cypress/CFISD area (Houston metro)
recent_news: BBB lists business as out of business. Incorporated 10/8/2008 in Cypress, TX. No recent M&A or operational activity confirmed.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate. Appears to be a defunct micro child-shuttle company (~$120K revenue, 4 employees historically), not a school-bus contractor. Drop from target list.

name: Curl School Bus Svc
principals: Robert (Rob) M. Curl, owner (third-generation); wife Sara Curl
districts_or_customers: Vernonia School District (Oregon) - sole contractor under multi-year district contract since 1978
recent_news: Featured by Oregon Coast Bank (~2020) as one of only three small local school-bus contractors still serving Oregon districts; pivoted to lunch delivery during COVID and invested in fleet disinfection equipment.
fit_notes: Classic third-generation family-owned single-district contractor (19 buses, Vernonia SD). Too small to be a standalone tuck-in for Summit (sub-scale, rural NW Oregon), but could be folded into a broader PNW roll-up if Summit builds Oregon density. Low urgency; succession only medium since current owner is mid-career.

name: Rocky Top Tours Inc
districts_or_customers: Schools (field trips/away games), military groups, Smokies tourism (Pigeon Forge/Gatlinburg), Savannah Bananas event shuttles
recent_news: No M&A news. Active UMA member; USDOT 602917; SAM-registered government vendor. A related entity, Rocky Top School Buses LLC (USDOT 3408408, Maryville), exists as a separate motor carrier.
fit_notes: Predominantly a charter/motorcoach + Smokies tourism operator with school-charter as a secondary line; school contract revenue is likely modest. The separate Rocky Top School Buses LLC is more interesting if it holds home-to-school contracts. Marginal Summit fit unless TN charter+school adjacency is desired.

name: Wyshinski Bus Svc Inc
principals: Frank E. Wyshinski (President), John E. Wyshinski (VP) - 3rd generation since 2008; Wendy Green (GM); founded by John J. Wyshinski (grandfather)
districts_or_customers: Pennsville, Penns Grove, Carneys Point Regional and other districts in Salem and Gloucester Counties, NJ
recent_news: No recent M&A. Acquired Parvin's Transit in 1988 (historical). Operates ~80-year family business; 3rd-generation leaders Frank and John E. took over in 2008.
fit_notes: Attractive Summit tuck-in: independent 3rd-generation NJ family operator since 1945, ~50+ employees, multi-district home-to-school in Salem/Gloucester Counties (an underserved corner of South Jersey for Summit). Brothers in charge since 2008 may be approaching retirement window - worth a direct outreach. Note registered street is 460 Hollywood Ave Carneys Point; physical yard often shown at 12 E Griffith St, Penns Grove.
